mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency during rate cuts falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered months, or the t-statistic for return difference versus baseline is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5 percent month-over-month during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity utilization tightening, prompting expectations of supply constraints and margin compression that weigh on equity valuations.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods, or regime classification yields no predictive power (AUC < 0.52).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, reinforcing baseline equity risk premiums and generating positive short-term market momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following GDP prints above 2.0% show no significant deviation from market baseline (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating price pressures that force markets to price in higher future discount rates, generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered months, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all out-of-sample testing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening and wage inflation pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and produce negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields a positive or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample testing, or 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of divergent months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real sales of final goods exceeding 0.3 percent during a monetary easing cycle signals that accommodative policy fails to stimulate consumption, triggering risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances during easing periods, or regime-conditional predictive accuracy drops below 52%.
